title: aralia elata var. inermis
intro: Aralia elata var. inermis, also known as Japanese angelica tree, is a plant species in the Araliaceae family, characterized by its large, compound leaves and ability to grow up to 10 meters tall. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and coumarins. The health benefits of Aralia elata var. inermis include its potential to reduce blood pressure, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of menopause. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and stems are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide relief from various health issues.
